spec_match.rationale: Models the real shipping pattern â€” a cart-mutation response carrying `Clear-Site-Data: "prefetchCache", "prerenderCache"` invalidates speculative copies, and the page distinguishes between prefetch-stale and prerender-stale correctly.

interactive.rationale: Add/remove cart buttons, warm-speculative button, navigate button, and an opt-in toggle for the server header all drive a live log and live pill states. Comfortably interactive.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises Shopify-style cart-mutation invalidation with speculative pills flipping staleâ†’cleared; the page delivers that.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers stale-vs-fresh and the opt-out path, but misses what the spec calls out: when only the rendered state is stale (clear `prerenderCache` only) vs when the HTML itself is stale (`prefetchCache`). The when-to-clear-which prose is there but the buttons don't let you pick a single tier.

references.rationale: Links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/4755745652080640 in see-also, plus the Shopify performance blog as primary source.

design_system.rationale: Mostly clean â€” variables throughout, button overrides include both background and color â€” but `.warn-block` sets both `background: var(--accent-amber)` and `color: var(--accent-amber)` inside `strong` which renders amber-on-amber and likely fails WCAG AA. Needs `color: var(--text-black)` (or a darker accent) on the strong rule.

openQuestions.title: Fix the amber-on-amber `.warn-block strong` rule
openQuestions.detail: `.warn-block` sets background to `var(--accent-amber)` and then `.warn-block strong { color: var(--accent-amber); }` paints the same colour on top â€” invisible. Either remove the strong colour or set it to `var(--text-black)`. Real contrast failure, not a nit.

openQuestions.title: Add a `prerenderCache` only button
openQuestions.detail: The page's own copy talks about when to clear prerender-only vs prefetch-only, but the demo always sends both. Add two extra buttons (or radio toggles on the cart-add request) that emit each token alone so the visitor can see the prefetch pill survive while prerender flips, and vice versa â€” that's the spec's most-misunderstood point.

openQuestions.title: Show what the response does NOT clear
openQuestions.detail: A side-panel listing of cookies / HTTP cache / IndexedDB with `untouched` badges would make the whole point of the feature concrete (the surgical clear vs the `*` hammer). Right now the user has to take the prose on faith.

openQuestions.title: Add a navigation-while-mutating race
openQuestions.detail: A real edge case: prerender is active, user clicks the cart-mutation button, the response carries Clear-Site-Data. The spec says active prerenders are skipped â€” model this with a button labelled `Navigate while mutating` and show the prerender activate even though the next prerender warm would have been cleared.

summary: The strongest of the four concepts on this feature â€” anchored in a real production pattern, with a live log and pill states that actually teach. The contrast bug on the warn block is the most pressing fix; after that, surfacing single-token responses and the active-prerender skip would round out coverage.
